scenario: Catalyst Marketplace runs a multi-tenant Camunda 8.8 deployment for 35 tenants and 21000 jobs per hour. The team is migrating away from the legacy camunda.client.tenant-ids key listed in the deprecated camunda.client table and must adopt the documented 8.8 replacement under the unified worker configuration.
question: Which property does the 8.8 reference list as the replacement for the legacy camunda.client.tenant-ids key?

options.text: The 8.8 reference replaces the legacy camunda.client.tenant-ids key with camunda.client.worker.defaults.tenant-ids under the Spring Boot starter properties table.
The 8.8 reference replaces the legacy camunda.client.tenant-ids key with camunda.client.cloud.tenant-ids under the Spring Boot starter properties table.
The 8.8 reference replaces the legacy camunda.client.tenant-ids key with camunda.client.auth.tenant-ids under the Spring Boot starter properties table.
The 8.8 reference replaces the legacy camunda.client.tenant-ids key with camunda.client.worker.override.tenant-ids under the starter properties table.

a.text: Correct. The camunda.client table maps camunda.client.tenant-ids to camunda.client.worker.defaults.tenant-ids as the documented 8.8 replacement key.

b.text: Incorrect. The camunda.client.cloud section deals with SaaS cluster details and does not contain a tenant-ids replacement key in 8.8.

c.text: Incorrect. camunda.client.auth groups OAuth and basic auth properties; the tenant-ids replacement lives under the worker defaults block.

d.text: Incorrect. The override block is keyed by job type, not by a single tenant-ids entry; the documented replacement is the defaults block.

explanation: The deprecated camunda.client table in the 8.8 reference points camunda.client.tenant-ids to its replacement camunda.client.worker.defaults.tenant-ids, which feeds the default tenant list for every registered worker.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
